title: Maya Is Really Bad At Her Job
display_title: Maya Is Really Bad At Her Job
wikitext:
[]: Maya Is Really Bad At Her Job             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Mon Mar 21, 2011 1:37 am Seriously. She questions everything. EVERYTHING. By the 50th "But sir, if we do that..." , I'm amazed Gendo doesn't go down there and slap a bitch. Also, someone that breaks down and covers her face when she sees a monster fight probably shouldn't take jobs that involve monitoring giant monster fights.

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Mon Mar 21, 2011 2:35 am But she's cute! Though in all seriousness, I did feel quite bad for Maya during EoE. Between Asuka's battle, the invasion of Nerv, and the appearance of GNR, she didn't seem to handle things very well.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Mon Mar 21, 2011 2:40 am When I saw this topic all I could think about was reading out the first letter of each word that's been capitalised. Maya Is Really Bad At Her Job. MIRBAHJ . Also, I disagree. She's a computer programmer and technician, not a mecha battle coach or a giant humanoid hygenist - those are Ritsuko's jobs.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Mon Mar 21, 2011 2:50 am When Nerv's top pilot is literally having the life squeezed out of him, her job is NOT to argue with superiors about what to do! I mean, Gendo has to convince her , for God's sake! She treats everyone like an idiot. "But sir, if we [do obvious thing], it means [she thinks you're a moron who hasn't been paying attention]." Yes Maya, they know. rhfxz1s wrote: Between Asuka's battle, the invasion of Nerv, and the appearance of GNR, she didn't seem to handle things very well.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Mon Mar 21, 2011 3:19 am Warren Peace wrote: When Nerv's top pilot is literally having the life squeezed out of him, her job is NOT to argue with superiors about what to do! I mean, Gendo has to convince her , for God's sake!

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon Mar 21, 2011 5:28 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Mon Mar 21, 2011 6:12 am Absolutely, from a plot mechanics standpoint, the questioning of her superiors is to make sure the audience is clear on things. But within the context of the story, she's a liability. Your comments about her connection to Ritsuko are spot on. Akagi would let a lot go in exchange for the ego boost Maya provides. Still, this calls into question Gendo's character as well. I just don't see him suffering a "fool" (as he would no doubt think it) like Maya. He wants someone who will push the button, not make an argument for why this other button is the better idea.

[]:             Quote Post by Jessemon » Mon Mar 21, 2011 7:59 am Warren Peace wrote: Absolutely, from a plot mechanics standpoint, the questioning of her superiors is to make sure the audience is clear on things. But within the context of the story, she's a liability. Your comments about her connection to Ritsuko are spot on. Akagi would let a lot go in exchange for the ego boost Maya provides. Still, this calls into question Gendo's character as well. I just don't see him suffering a "fool" (as he would no doubt think it) like Maya. He wants someone who will push the button, not make an argument for why this other button is the better idea.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Mar 21, 2011 9:44 am Anything before that is debatable, but you can't blame anyone for losing it in FREAKIN EOE. - They can't all be super professional like Ritsuko or Misato...

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Mar 21, 2011 9:56 am I <3 this thread.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Mon Mar 21, 2011 10:30 am As Shin-seiki likes to point out: Aoba berates Maya for cowering under her desk when the soldiers are invading, but when the Reis come for him, he does the same thing while she is still at her computer.

[]:             Quote Post by SHINOBI-03 » Mon Mar 21, 2011 10:37 am How many times did she succeed with auto-ejecting the entry plugs? I know at least once.

[]:             Quote Post by Jessemon » Mon Mar 21, 2011 10:39 am thewayneiac wrote: As Shin-seiki likes to point out: Aoba berates Maya for cowering under her desk when the soldiers are invading, but when the Reis come for him, he does the same thing while she is still at her computer.

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Mon Mar 21, 2011 10:47 am Kendrix wrote: Anything before that is debatable, but you can't blame anyone for losing it in FREAKIN EOE. - They can't all be super professional like Ritsuko or Misato...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Mar 21, 2011 1:12 pm Warren Peace wrote: Absolutely, from a plot mechanics standpoint, the questioning of her superiors is to make sure the audience is clear on things. But within the context of the story, she's a liability.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Mon Mar 21, 2011 1:42 pm Warren Peace wrote: Also, someone that breaks down and covers her face when she sees a monster fight probably shouldn't take jobs that involve monitoring giant monster fights.

[]:             Quote Post by DevRei17 » Mon Mar 21, 2011 3:18 pm Merridian wrote: Her job seemed to entail boring diagnostic work and technical maintenance on the Magi more than it did supervising the destruction of the Angels. And given NERV's penchant for secrecy, I doubt "monitoring the dismemberment of kaiju at the hands of teenagers in mecha" was in the job description she applied for.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon Mar 21, 2011 4:31 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Light » Mon Mar 21, 2011 4:45 pm You forgot the extremely weak stomach part.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Mon Mar 21, 2011 4:45 pm Maya is bad at her job, just like Gendo is bad with relationships, just like Asuka is bad at communicating, just like Shinji is bad at accepting himself, just like Rei is bad at emoting and so on...... It's dysfunction junction, people. You're not allowed on the show unless you have glaring weaknesses for the writers to exploit mercilessly.

Also, someone that breaks down and covers her face when she sees a monster fight probably shouldn't take jobs that involve monitoring giant monster fights.
